ac_evidence.ac: QualityTier enum with numeric values (0=Poor, 4=Masterwork) and string representations
Quality effects: tool durability (Poor: 0.5x, Masterwork: 3.0x), production speed modifiers, product value multipliers
ac_evidence.evidence: quality.go:DurabilityMultiplier, ProductionSpeedMultiplier, resources.go:ComputeValue, TestDurabilityMultiplier, TestProductionSpeedMultiplier, TestComputeValue

ac_evidence.ac: Spoilage resistance: higher quality reduces spoilage rate (Good: 0.7x, Masterwork: 0.3x)
Craftsmanship: worker skill level determines achievable quality (novice: Poor-Normal, expert: Good-Excellent, master: Masterwork chance)
Quality inheritance in production: output quality = weighted average of input quality + skill bonus
Special materials enable higher quality (fine iron, seasoned wood)
Skill progression: workers improve skills through practice, teaching possible
Quality visible in UI (icons, tooltips planned for frontend workstream)
ac_evidence.evidence: Frontend workstream; backend provides quality tier string representation

ac_evidence.ac: Unit tests covering quality calculations and skill effects

existing_work_summary: QualityTier enum, QualityToFloat/FloatToQuality, computeOutputQuality, skill references in recipes. Extended with quality effects, craftsmanship, skill definitions, special material bonuses.
